title: Podman `internal` network issue
body:
### Issue Description

So I am trying to communicate between different rootless containers.

So I am running a database in a container and exposing its port by port mapping.
Also I have another container for my application to connect with the same db and its working fine.

But there is a catch that I don't want to have internet connection in my application so I applied `internal` network with `no-dns` as soon as I add this db becomes unreachable from the application.

Things I have tried :
- adding internal network to both db and application didn't worked
- running both in open network works seemlessly
- setting host as `$(podman inspect database -f '{{ .NetworkSettings.IPAddress }}')` also failed

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
```bash
podman network create --internal internal-no-dns-internet
podman pull mongo:latest
podman run  --cpu-shares=768  --memory=2g -d -p 27017:27017 --name=database mongo
while true; do
    dbReady=$(podman logs database | grep -nE "Waiting for connections")
    if [ ! -z "$dbReady" ] ; then
        break
    fi
    sleep 1
done
podman run --name=backend-exec --cpu-shares=512 --network internal-no-dns-internet --detach --cap-drop ALL --security-opt no-new-privileges  executor
```


### Describe the results you received

Connection refused

### Describe the results you expected

Properly established connection between containers

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Observed the above both in linux and macOs

### Additional information

It happens only with internal network.

comments.body:
Seems to work fine for me:
```shell
$ podman network create --internal internal
internal
$ podman run --name con1 --network internal -d alpine top
a4e0eb8b7973c8ab8eec2483121bb1a98834e64a67414d6a67685720844406b8
$ podman run --rm --network internal alpine nslookup con1
Server:         10.89.2.1
Address:        10.89.2.1:53

Non-authoritative answer:
Name:   con1.dns.podman
Address: 10.89.2.2

Non-authoritative answer:
```


> podman run  --cpu-shares=768  --memory=2g -d -p 27017:27017 --name=database mongo

Maybe a typo in the issue but this command is missing the --network option.

2 things

- If I use the default network for database then its not available to use from other containers with internal network
- Also if I disable dns is there a way we can communicate between containers?

If the containers should communicate you should put them in the same network. Note that you can assign more than one network to a container, i.e.  use `--network podman --network internal-no-dns-internet` for your mongo container when it should be able to connect to the internal one and have a connection to the outside.
